heading: Exceptions to arbitral awards
label: 7122 Exceptions to arbitral awards
notes: Amendments1984—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally, substituting “beginning on the date the award is served on the party” for “beginning on the date of such award”.Pub. L. 98–224
Effective DateSection effective 90 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1978 Amendment note under .Oct. 13, 1978section 907 of Pub. L. 95–454section 1101 of this title
amendments: Amendments1984—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally, substituting “beginning on the date the award is served on the party” for “beginning on the date of such award”.Pub. L. 98–224
Effective DateSection effective 90 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1978 Amendment note under .Oct. 13, 1978section 907 of Pub. L. 95–454section 1101 of this title

children.text: because it is contrary to any law, rule, or regulation; or
on other grounds similar to those applied by Federal courts in private sector labor-management relations;

children.label: b
children.text: If no exception to an arbitrator’s award is filed under subsection (a) of this section during the 30-day period beginning on the date the award is served on the party, the award shall be final and binding. An agency shall take the actions required by an arbitrator’s final award. The award may include the payment of backpay (as provided in ).section 5596 of this title
